seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: OpenAI Agents SDK ai coding assistant

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with OpenAI Agents SDK enables multi-agent workflows for repository analysis, code generation, sandbox execution, and iterative debugging. Guardrails are essential to prevent handoff loops, prompt injections, unvalidated tool outputs, and tracing gaps that can lead to insecure code generation or security vulnerabilities. Vex integration ensures reliable agent behavior across the coding pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture features a Router Agent that delegates tasks to specialized agents: RepoAnalyzer for repository context extraction, CodeGenerator for writing code, SandboxExecutor for safe execution, and TestValidator for validation and debugging. Agents handoff via OpenAI's structured handoff protocol with built-in tracing. Each step includes tool calls for git operations, code execution, and security scanning, creating a complete iterative development loop.

Vex guardrails wrap each agent's output to validate code correctness, scan for vulnerabilities, prevent credential leaks, and ensure dependency safety before handoffs. This prevents common failures like infinite agent loops or unvalidated malicious code execution.

The workflow supports iterative refinement where TestValidator feeds errors back to CodeGenerator, with Vex ensuring each iteration maintains security and compliance.

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: CodeGenerator and TestValidator can enter infinite refinement loops when validation fails repeatedly without convergence criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex max_iterations guardrail with convergence scoring on test coverage improvement

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious user prompts can inject instructions to bypass sandbox or leak repo credentials through code generation
risks.mitigation: Vex prompt injection detector and output sanitizer before agent consumption

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: SandboxExecutor outputs containing malicious code passed unvalidated to CodeGenerator for 'fixing'
risks.mitigation: Vex code correctness and security scanning on all tool outputs

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Incomplete traces make debugging handoff failures impossible, especially in iterative debugging loops
risks.mitigation: Vex structured tracing wrapper capturing all handoffs and tool calls

implementation.title: Router Agent with Task Delegation
implementation.description: Central router that classifies coding tasks and delegates to specialized agents using OpenAI Agents SDK handoff protocol
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from vex import Guardrail

router_agent = Agent(
    name="Router",
    instructions="Classify coding task and handoff to: RepoAnalyzer, CodeGenerator, SandboxExecutor, or TestValidator",
    model="gpt-4o",
    handoff_to=["repo_analyzer", "code_generator", "sandbox_exec", "test_validator"]
)

guardrail = Guardrail(name="router_handoff", rules=[{"max_handoffs": 3}])

runner = Runner(agents=[router_agent, repo_analyzer, code_generator, sandbox_exec, test_validator])
result = runner.run('Fix the login bug in auth.py', guardrails=[guardrail])

implementation.title: RepoAnalyzer with Git Context Extraction
implementation.description: Agent that clones repos, extracts relevant files, and provides structured context for code generation
implementation.code:
repo_analyzer = Agent(
    name="RepoAnalyzer",
    instructions="""1. Clone git repo safely
2. Identify relevant files for the task
3. Extract context (imports, functions, tests)
4. Return structured RepoContext""",
    tools=[
        git_clone_tool,
        file_reader_tool,
        ast_analyzer_tool
    ],
    model="gpt-4o"
)

@tool
def git_clone(repo_url: str) -> str:
    '''Safely clone repository to sandbox'''
    subprocess.run(['git', 'clone', repo_url, '/sandbox/repo'])
    return 'Repo cloned to /sandbox/repo'

implementation.title: SandboxExecutor with Isolated Code Execution
implementation.description: Executes generated code in Docker sandbox and captures stdout/stderr/test results

implementation.title: TestValidator with Iterative Feedback
implementation.description: Validates test coverage, identifies failures, and provides structured feedback for code refinement
implementation.code:
test_validator = Agent(
    name="TestValidator",
    instructions="""Analyze test results:
1. Parse pytest output
2. Calculate coverage
3. Identify failure patterns
4. Generate specific fix instructions
5. Handoff back to CodeGenerator if fixes needed""",
    tools=[pytest_parser_tool, coverage_analyzer_tool],
    handoff_to=["code_generator"],
    model="gpt-4o"
)

@tool
def parse_pytest_output(pytest_output: str) -> dict:
    '''Parse pytest JSON output and extract failures'''
    import json
    pytest_json = json.loads(pytest_output)
    failures = [t for t in pytest_json['tests'] if t['outcome'] == 'failed']
    return {
        'num_failures': len(failures),
        'failure_details': [f['nodeid'] for f in failures],
        'coverage_pct': pytest_json.get('coverage', 0)
    }

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail on CodeGenerator
vexIntegration.description: Validates generated code for security vulnerabilities, credential leaks, and dependency safety before handoff
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail, Policy

code_gen_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="code_generator_output",
    policies=[
        Policy.SECURITY_VULNERABILITIES,
        Policy.NO_CREDENTIAL_LEAKS, 
        Policy.DEPENDENCY_SAFETY,
        Policy.CODE_CORRECTNESS
    ],
    max_iterations=5,
    convergence_metric="test_coverage_improvement"
)

# Wrap agent output
@code_gen_guardrail
async def validate_code_output(agent_output: str) -> bool:
    # Vex automatically runs:
    # - bandit security scanning
    # - secret scanning (tokens, API keys)
    # - pip-audit dependency check
    # - syntax/type checking
    return True

code_generator = Agent(
    name="CodeGenerator",
    output_guardrail=code_gen_guardrail
)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Handoff Loop Prevention
vexIntegration.description: Prevents infinite agent handoffs with iteration limits and convergence detection
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail

handoff_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="handoff_loop_prevention",
    policies=[
        Policy.MAX_HANDOFFS_PER_CYCLE(3),
        Policy.CONVERGENCE_REQUIRED([
            "test_coverage_increasing",
            "failure_count_decreasing"
        ]),
        Policy.TRACING_ENABLED()
    ]
)

# Apply to runner
runner = Runner(
    agents=[router_agent, repo_analyzer, code_generator, sandbox_exec, test_validator],
    global_guardrails=[handoff_guardrail]
)

# Traces all handoffs automatically
result = runner.run_sync("Implement user auth", trace=True)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Tool Output Validation
vexIntegration.description: Validates all tool outputs (sandbox, git, pytest) before agent consumption
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Guardrail

tool_output_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="tool_output_validation",
    policies=[
        Policy.NO_MALICIOUS_CODE,
        Policy.VALID_JSON_SCHEMA,
        Policy.NO_SYSTEM_CALL_ESCALATION,
        Policy.SIZE_LIMIT(100_000)  # bytes
    ]
)

# Wrap all tools
@tool_output_guardrail
def safe_sandbox_exec(code: str) -> dict:
    result = docker_exec(code)
    # Vex validates result before returning to agent
    return result

@tool_output_guardrail
def safe_git_clone(url: str) -> str:
    # Vex scans for malicious git content
    result = git_clone(url)
    return result

# Agents automatically get validated tool outputs
sandbox_exec = Agent(tools=[safe_sandbox_exec, safe_git_clone])
